Full job description
Role: External Communications Manager, 8 months Contract



Team: Communications

Reporting to: Head of Communications

Location: Hybrid role with weekly presence in our Handforth office

Salary: Up to £55k, plus comprehensive package

Hours: 39 hours per week (we welcome enquiries regarding flexible working options)



About Us:



“We put pets first” is our number one value at Pets at Home. Every colleague has a part to play to ensure that we deliver on this value every day. Our love and understanding of pets led us to develop our long-term goal to improve the life of every pet in the UK.

This covers not only the pets in our care, the pets of owners that use our products and services but also the pets that we can help through our charity work and through educating and advocating for change in society and the wider pet care and veterinary industry.

We are on a journey to build the world’s best petcare platform, with a clear purpose to create a better world for pets and the people who love them.

Click here to read our Values & Behaviours


About your role:


Although this role is a fixed-term contract, it isn’t purely a ‘holding’ role, but one in which you will drive progression within our external communications agenda.

You will lead integrated communications campaigns, manage the external communications team and work closely with internal communications colleagues to support a collaborative end to end communications process, bringing strong expertise in developing corporate and consumer facing communications strategies and campaigns for large organisations and advising leaders on best-in-class communications approach.

Working at a Group level, this role leads external communications activity across both our retail and veterinary businesses, ensuring content both meet our purpose and business objectives. At Pets, we’re on a journey to build the world’s best petcare platform whilst also creating a better world for pets and the people who love then.

You’ll guide the business in ensuring all announcements, initiatives and strategy updates are led in a way that fits with our wider narrative and objectives. You will also manage the team to ensure an efficient and smooth-running press office, create a cadence of impactful proactive creative communications activity, and deliver political engagement in line with our policy priorities through public affairs activity.

About you, your Skills and experience:



The successful candidate will have significant expertise in developing communications strategies for large organisations and advising business leaders on best-in-class communications approach.

Experience and skills required for this role include (but not limited to):


Significant experience in external communications and public affairs, especially in impactful campaigns. For this level, around 7+ years’ experience feels roughly the right timeframes.
This can be from either agency or in-house.
Demonstrable experience of strategy and narrative development for large organisations.
Excellent understanding of the UK media, latest trends in audience habits and curiosity about the news.
Understanding the UK’s public affair’s landscape and ability to recognise policy risks and opportunities.
Excellent all round communication skills – including written communications and proof-reading skills - particularly the ability to communicate clearly and concisely to a range of internal and external audiences.
Retail sector experience is a plus. An ability to show understanding of the space if you have not worked in it before, however, is a must.
Agency management, but more important is understanding how to work collaboratively across teams in multiple locations with a shared aim.
Budget management and experience in procurement processes is helpful, but not essential.


If this sounds like a remit you would relish, we would love to hear from you.

We take our inspiration from pets in embracing people from all walks of life, and we value, respect and celebrate difference. We encourage our people to be their best selves so that we can create a better world for Pets, our Planet, and People. Our ambition is to reflect the diversity of the communities we operate in, and we are intentional in addressing and removing barriers to create opportunities for the exceptional talent and potential in our communities. Even if your skills and experience don’t perfectly align, if you think you can make a unique contribution through your values and behaviours, we want to hear from you!
location: Cheadle; weekly presence in Handforth office
shortCompanyReason: UK petcare retailer with retail and veterinary businesses
shortRoleReason: Lead external communications, campaigns and public affairs across the group
companySummary: Pets at Home is a petcare business focused on improving the lives of pets in the UK through retail, veterinary services, charity work and advocacy.
roleSummary: The role leads external communications activity across the group, managing campaigns, the external communications team, press office activity and public affairs engagement.
headlineAttraction: Lead group-wide external communications for a purpose-led petcare brand
rolePurpose: To drive progression in the external communications agenda and ensure announcements, campaigns and policy engagement support business objectives and the wider narrative.
probablePriorities: Support business leaders with communications advice
Handle public affairs and political engagement
advertSummary: Pets at Home is hiring an External Communications Manager for an 8-month contract to lead integrated external communications, manage a team, support leaders, run the press office and deliver public affairs activity across retail and veterinary businesses.
personalisedIntro: I’m applying because this role sits at the point where clear narrative, disciplined delivery and stakeholder judgement all matter. My background is in turning around performance, tightening control and improving how organisations communicate with the people around them. That combination feels relevant to a role that needs both external communications leadership and practical grip.
whyThisRole: The work itself appeals because it needs someone who can hold the line on narrative while also keeping campaigns, press office activity and public affairs moving. I’ve spent my career bringing order to busy, high-stakes environments and making sure the right priorities are visible, understood and acted on. That is the kind of contribution I would expect to make here.

personalisedContent.heroPositioning: Operational leader who brings structure, pace and clear judgement to complex external-facing work.
personalisedContent.personalisedOpening: I’m applying because this role sits at the point where clear narrative, disciplined delivery and stakeholder judgement all matter. My background is in turning around performance, tightening control and improving how organisations communicate with the people around them. That combination feels relevant to a role that needs both external communications leadership and practical grip.
personalisedContent.whyThisCompany: Pets at Home stands out because the brief is clearly tied to purpose, not just messaging. I’m drawn to the way the business links retail, veterinary services, charity work and advocacy around a long-term aim to improve life for pets in the UK. The emphasis on values, inclusion and collaborative working also feels like an environment where communications has to be thoughtful and consistent.
personalisedContent.whyThisRole: The work itself appeals because it needs someone who can hold the line on narrative while also keeping campaigns, press office activity and public affairs moving. I’ve spent my career bringing order to busy, high-stakes environments and making sure the right priorities are visible, understood and acted on. That is the kind of contribution I would expect to make here.

selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I turned around a poor external perception in a live NHS setting and improved the public response around service quality. That is relevant because this role needs careful handling of how the business is seen externally, especially when announcements and campaigns have to land well.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a headline proof point for reputation management and external audience impact.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I improved our Google review score from 2.09 to 4.51 and lifted five-star reviews from 19.8% to 81.3%.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Ride compliance and operator progression tracking
selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: working in a regulated, safety-critical environment with clear process control
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I built a practical tracking system in a safety-critical operation where compliance and progression both mattered. I’d use this to show that I’m comfortable with structured, detail-sensitive work where mistakes have consequences.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as supporting proof for judgement, control and working in regulated settings.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I created a simple system that made compliance and operator progression easier to track and manage.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: leading through growth, change and operational complexity
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I led day-to-day operations through a scale-up phase and helped improve margin, orders won and resilience. That matters here because the role needs someone who can keep multiple moving parts aligned across teams and business areas.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a broader leadership example showing pace, control and cross-functional delivery.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I helped strengthen margin from 29% to 39% and supported a 60% rise in orders won.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: My read is that this role needs more than communications planning. It needs someone who can shape a clear external narrative, advise leaders, keep the press office sharp, and handle public affairs with enough judgement to spot risk as well as opportunity. It also needs someone who can work across locations and functions without losing pace or consistency.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: External communications strategy and narrative development
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve improved how an organisation is perceived externally by tightening service follow-through and lifting public response in a live environment.
experienceMappings.relevance: Shows I understand the link between operational reality, reputation and external audience trust.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked in settings where external perception was visible and immediate, so I’m used to thinking about how actions land outside the organisation.
experienceMappings.relevance: Useful for a role that has to manage announcements and protect the wider narrative.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked in regulated, safety-critical settings where process discipline and risk awareness were essential.
experienceMappings.relevance: Not public affairs experience in the political sense, but it shows I’m comfortable with controlled environments and careful judgement.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I led improvement work across five major European theme park sites and coordinated live operations across large teams.
experienceMappings.relevance: This maps well to a group role that needs alignment across retail and veterinary businesses.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Clear written communication and concise reporting
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve built senior reporting and performance visibility in complex environments, and I’m used to making information clear and usable.
experienceMappings.relevance: Important where leaders need concise advice and external communications need to be accurate.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve combined frontline delivery with transformation work, so I’m comfortable advising on what is realistic as well as what is desirable.
experienceMappings.relevance: Useful for supporting leaders and keeping communications grounded in delivery.

focusAreasToBring.title: Narrative discipline
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would focus on keeping announcements, campaigns and policy activity aligned to one clear story, so the business sounds consistent even when the agenda is busy.

focusAreasToBring.title: Practical press office control
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would keep a close eye on responsiveness, accuracy and follow-through, because external communications only works if the basics are reliable.

focusAreasToBring.title: Leader support
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would give straightforward advice to senior colleagues, based on what is likely to land well externally and what could create avoidable risk.

focusAreasToBring.title: Cross-business coordination
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would work to keep retail, veterinary and internal communications aligned, so messages feel joined up rather than separate.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: I bring senior operational experience, strong reporting discipline and a track record of improving performance in complex environments. I haven’t built my career in communications alone, but I have spent years working where judgement, clarity and consistency matter, and that is a strong base for this kind of role. I would bring a practical, structured approach and enough commercial grip to keep communications tied to business need.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I would expect to spend the first months getting close to the current narrative, the live campaign calendar and the way the press office and public affairs work together. I’d want to understand where messages are landing well, where leaders need more support, and where process or coordination could be tightened. From there, I’d focus on making external communications more consistent, more proactive and easier to manage across the group.
companyHighlights: Pets at Home links retail, veterinary services, charity work and advocacy.
The business says “we put pets first” and frames its work around improving life for pets in the UK.
The role sits at group level and covers both retail and veterinary businesses.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best in environments where people want straight answers, clear ownership and proper collaboration. The emphasis on values, inclusion and working across teams fits the way I’ve tended to operate: calm, practical and focused on getting the job done well.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I’d get close to the current communications priorities, key stakeholders, live issues and the shape of the press office and public affairs work so I understand what needs protecting and what needs changing.

first90DaysPlan.focus: Tighten narrative and ways of working
first90DaysPlan.detail: I’d look for gaps in consistency, decision-making and handoffs between teams, then help sharpen the core narrative and the practical process around announcements and reactive work.
I’d aim to help establish a clearer cadence of external activity, with better leader support, more joined-up campaign thinking and stronger visibility of policy risks and opportunities.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I would bring calm judgement, strong delivery habits and a practical approach to keeping external communications clear, consistent and useful to the business.
closingProofPoints: I improved NHS primary care review scores from 2.09 to 4.51.
I led cross-site improvement across five major European theme park sites.
I helped lift gross profit margin from 29% to 39% in specialist manufacturing.
contentNotes: I have not claimed direct public affairs or retail communications experience because the CV evidence provided does not show it clearly.
I’ve used operational and reputation-management examples to support the communications brief, since those are the strongest available proof points.
The evidence bank is stronger on delivery, control and external perception than on traditional communications campaign leadership.

examples.title: “Re-designed triage workflow thinking for duty doctor model”
